title: The First Year I Never Talk About

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>My first year as a mom <break time="350ms"/>nearly broke me. <break time="300ms"/>I don't talk about it much.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>Diego didn't sleep. <break time="300ms"/>I didn't sleep. <break time="500ms"/>I remember standing in the shower at 4am, <break time="400ms"/>crying, <break time="350ms"/>while my husband walked the baby downstairs. <break time="500ms"/>I googled things at 3am that I was ashamed to type. <break time="400ms"/>'Is it normal to regret having a baby.' <break time="350ms"/>'What if I don't love my baby.' <break time="300ms"/>'Can you give your baby back.' <break time="500ms"/>I felt like a monster. <break time="400ms"/>Like I was broken. <break time="350ms"/>Like everyone else knew how to do this <break time="300ms"/>and I was the only one drowning.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>It took me three years <break time="400ms"/>to tell anyone about those feelings. <break time="350ms"/>Three years. <break time="500ms"/>So if you're having dark thoughts - <break time="400ms"/>thoughts that scare you - <break time="350ms"/>I need you to know: <break time="300ms"/>that doesn't make you a bad parent. <break time="500ms"/>It makes you a human <break time="400ms"/>going through something impossible. <break time="350ms"/>And it can get better. <break time="300ms"/>It got better for me.

when_to_share: When someone admits to dark thoughts, regret, or struggling to bond with their child
